This Residential Purchase and Sale Agreement is made and entered into as of November 17, 2023, by and between Katherine Nguyen (“BUYER”) and Debra Smith (“SELLER”). EARNEST MONEY DEPOSIT BUYER has paid or shall pay an earnest money deposit of $10,000.00, to be held in escrow by Nova Title Company and credited toward the purchase price at closing. The deposits received under this Agreement are itemized below. EARNEST MONEY / DEPOSIT SCHEDULE ITEM DEPOSIT TYPE CHECK # / REFERENCE DATE AMOUNT 1 Reservation Deposit 433 11/19/2023 $5,000.00 2 10% Contract Deposit 434 12/01/2023 $2,500.00 3 10% Contract Deposit 435 12/13/2023 $5,000.00 Total Deposits $12,500.00 05 CLOSING AND POSSESSION Closing of this transaction shall occur on or before March 06, 2024 (or 3/6/2024) at the offices of Nova Title Company, or at such other time and place as the parties may mutually agree. SELLER shall deliver possession of the Property to BUYER at closing, free of all occupants, with all systems and fixtures in working order. 06 CONTINGENCIES (a) Financing Contingency. This Agreement is contingent upon BUYER obtaining a firm written mortgage loan financing in the approximate amount of $294,180.00 at prevailing market rates and terms. If BUYER cannot obtain such financing, this Agreement may be terminated and the earnest money shall be refunded. (b) Inspection Contingency. This Agreement is contingent upon BUYER's right, at BUYER's expense, to have the Property inspected within _____ days of the Effective Date of this Agreement. (c) Title Contingency. SELLER shall convey good and marketable title, free and clear of all encumbrances except those of record acceptable to BUYER. Title shall be insurable.

insured through Nova Title Company, BUYER may terminate this Agreement if title defects are not timely cured.

IF DEFAULT  
If BUYER defaults, SELLER may retain the earnest money as liquidated damages. If SELLER defaults, BUYER may pursue specific performance or recover the earnest money together with any actual damages permitted by law.

08 ACCEPTANCE AND SIGNATURES  
The parties acknowledge that they have read and understood this Agreement and agree to be bound by its terms. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties.

Consent to the Use of Electronic Records and Signatures (the "Consent") is provided by Rocket Mortgage, LLC ("we" or the "Lender") to Katherine Nygma ("you" or the "Borrower") under loan number 076548520. You accept this Consent by completing and submitting this Application as described in Section 2, Electronic Signatures, which provides your explicit confirmation and consent to use electronic records and signatures.

1. SCOPE OF YOUR CONSENT
Your consent applies to all records and communications that we, our affiliates, our service providers, and our successors and assigns are required or permitted to provide to you in connection with your loan. This includes, but is not limited to, disclosures, responses, notices, terms, agreements, and any other communications. You acknowledge that you can receive a paper copy of any Communications that we deliver to you electronically at any time.

2. ELECTRONIC SIGNATURES
You understand that your electronic signature(s) on application, disclosure acknowledgments, or other document provided to you in electronic form has the same legal effect as a written signature and is legally binding. When you click "I Agree," you are providing your consent and executing your application and affirming that you are able to receive communications in electronic form.

3. HARDWARE AND SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS
To access and retain the Communications, you must have:
- A personal computer or mobile device with internet access;
- A current web browser that supports the features necessary to access the Services;
- A valid email address; and
- A printer or storage device, if you wish to retain copies of the Communications.

4. CONFIRMING YOUR ABILITY TO ACCESS
Before you consent to receive Communications electronically, you should confirm that you are able to access information that we send to you in electronic form. You should ensure that your computer system meets the above requirements.

5. OBTAINING PAPER COPIES
You may request a paper copy of any Communication that we provide or make available to you electronically. To obtain a paper copy, you may contact us by telephone at 1-800-123-4567 or by writing to us at 1234 Clioine Street, Springfield, MA 12345.

In each case identifying yourself, referring to box number 4717617041, and specifying the Communications or Communications you wish to receive in paper form. We will send you the requested paper copy of communications. By signing below, you are instructing the recipient to provide you with the initial disclosures or other communications that were disclosed electronically only to you. If you do not respond promptly or otherwise to the messages you receive, it may affect the services we provide to you. You understand and agree that the responses you receive will be provided to you electronically and that the procedures described herein apply to all communications and disclosures herein.

1. KEEPING YOUR CONTACT INFORMATION CURRENT
You specifically agree to provide us with current and correct information, and to keep that information current. If your contact information is incorrect, you may not receive your communications or notices promptly. You agree to telephone us at 1-800-123-4567 and inform us of your current information.

2. WITHDRAWING YOUR CONSENT
You might have the ability to receive Communications electronically at any time. To withdraw your consent, you may send us by telephone at 1-800-123-4567. If you withdraw consent, you may not receive the electronic documents you desire. You have the option to request that Communications be sent in paper form.

3. FEDERAL AND STATE LAW GOVERNING THIS CONSENT
This Consent governs the delivery of Communications electronically as it may relate to your account and is intended to comply with the Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act of the United States. The Massachusetts Uniform Electronic Transactions Act and other applicable state provisions will also govern this Consent.

4. RECORDS RETENTION AND RELIABILITY
We will retain records for a period of time that documents you sign electronically or in a fashion that accurately reflects the information contained in each record and enables the record to be accurately reproduced for reference by all authorized parties under applicable state law.

5. ACKNOWLEDGMENT AND AGREEMENT
By providing your information and signing this consent under 4717617041, you acknowledge and agree that you have received and read this Consent, that you understand your rights in connection with this document, and that you consent to receiving electronic communications as noted. This Consent may not be amended except in writing.

This Consent is effective as of January 1, 2020 and will continue in effect with respect to your loan and your relationship with Rocket Mortgage, LLC until you withdraw your consent to receive communications in a format that is less effective, and/or in a form that provides an uncertainty concerning the receipt thereof.
Katherine Nguyen

This Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing Rights is provided to Katherine Sypion in connection with loan number 6721670053 secured by the property located at 5312 Cypress Glen, Minneapolis, MN 55418. All rights and responsibilities associated with the servicing of this loan have been transferred from your prior servicer to Rocket Mortgage, LLC. This Notice is for informational purposes only and does not change your loan terms or obligations. Please read carefully.

1. PURPOSE OF THIS NOTICE

You are receiving this Notice because the servicing of your mortgage loan, that is, the rights to receive and process your scheduled payments of principal, interest, and any other amounts due under your loan must be transferred. The transfer of servicing rights does not affect your mortgage loan and you are not being asked to do anything.

2. WHAT SERVICING MEANS

The term servicing refers to the collection and posting of your mortgage loan payment. A servicer is the entity responsible for collecting your monthly payment and managing your loan on behalf of the owner of the loan.

3. TRANSFER OF YOUR SERVICING

If servicing is transferred, the new servicer will send you a servicing notice that will inform you that your loan has been transferred to Rocket Mortgage, LLC. This notice will contain an effective date for the transfer of servicing rights and provide you with information regarding your new servicer.

4. IDENTIFY OF YOUR NEW SERVICER

Your new servicer is Rocket Mortgage, LLC, and will perform, manage, and service your loan compliantly, overseeing the servicing of your loan that will be directed to it.

Rocket Mortgage, LLC is committed to assisting you in managing your loan payments. The records, personal history, and account information regarding your loan will be transferred to Rocket Mortgage, LLC.

5. WHERE TO SEND YOUR PAYMENTS

Your loan payments should be made to the address given in Rocket Mortgage, LLC’s notice to you. Payment may be made to any of the following methods. Include sufficient payment along with your monthly payment. Payments must be sent via Rocket Mortgage, LLC’s acceptable means which will be spelled out clearly in the forthcoming notice provided by Rocket Mortgage, LLC.

Payments received by your prior servicer after the transfer date and submitted by Rocket Mortgage, LLC, may be subject to a processing delay. You should only pay us and communicate with us directly after the transfer date. If you have any questions regarding your servicing, you should contact Rocket Mortgage, LLC. If you are in doubt about the transfer date or your payments resulting from this transfer, please reach out directly to Rocket Mortgage, LLC for assistance.

6. SIXTY-DAY LATE FEE PROTECTION  
Payments received within 60 days of the transfer date will not be subject to a late fee. During the 60-day period beginning on the effective date of the transfer of servicing, your payments may not be processed as quickly as you expect. Please be assured that if you send your payment to your previous servicer in error or directly to us, your payment will still be applied to your loan account and we will not charge you a late fee as long as we receive your payment within that 60-day period.

To benefit from this protection, you must still make each payment in full on or before the due date shown on your loan documents. We cannot, however, guarantee that payments will arrive within the required time without appropriate forethought on your part. As a result, we recommend that you make your payments as early as possible during this period.

7. YOUR CONTINUING PAYMENT OBLIGATION  
The transfer of servicing does not affect, reduce, or otherwise alter your obligation to make timely payments on your loan and security instrument. Whether or not we have sent you a statement, you are required to continue to make your payments as scheduled. Likewise, you should continue to send payments to the address designated for such purpose until you are notified of a new address.

If you believe you are unable to make a scheduled payment, you should contact Rocket Mortgage, LLC at 1-800-1073 to discuss the options available to you.

8. ESCROW ACCOUNT AND INSURANCE  
If you have an escrow account with Rocket Mortgage, LLC for the payment of property taxes, hazard insurance premiums, mortgage insurance, or other charges, the administration of that escrow account may be performed by us, and we will notify you if there are any changes in the amounts collected in your escrow account or if the account is subject to new requirements or different conditions. You may contact us for information about analyzing your escrow account.

9. NOTICES OF ERROR AND INFORMATION REQUESTS  
If you believe there is an error in your account or if you want further information about your loan, you have the right to submit a written notice of error or a request for information. To do so, you must send your written request to Rocket Mortgage, LLC at 1-800-481-3307. Any notice of error or information request must be sent to us via U.S. mail, with sufficient detail to allow us to understand and respond to your request.

10. COMMUNICATIONS AND ACCOUNT INFORMATION  
On or after the transfer date, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will send you monthly billing statements, account updates, and other regulatory and compliance notices regarding your mortgage. These documents will also include our contact information, which you can use should you have questions or concerns that you wish to address. If you believe you have previously communicated with us and your inquiry has not been resolved, please feel free to contact us again.

In summary, your transfer of servicing to Rocket Mortgage, LLC does not change the overall terms of your loan and your responsibilities remain the same. Your prior servicer has provided this notice to help ensure you have accurate information on your mortgage. If you have questions regarding Rocket Mortgage, LLC, you may contact Rocket Mortgage, LLC directly at the contact information on the notice you received.

1.1. EFFECT ON LOAN TERMS AND LEGAL RIGHTS Except for the change in the identity of the servicer and place to which payments and correspondences are directed, the transfer of servicing has no effect on the terms of your loan or the rights and obligations of either party under the promissory note or the mortgage (security instrument) securing your loan. You should make your payments to the new servicer. Make sure to inform the new servicer of your payment history for any applicable payments. You can always contact the current servicer with any questions regarding your obligations or your loan documents. This notice is provided for informational purposes and does not alter any legal obligations that you or your loan servicer may have. If you have questions about how this transfer of servicing affects your particular circumstances, please reach out to your legal advisor or other appropriate professionals. Borski Mortgage, LLC is an equal housing lender that makes loans available to you, subject to reasonable acceptances and conditions based on your recent lending profile and financial.

This Schedule of Fees and Charges is furnished by Rocket Mortgage, LLC ("Lender", "we", "us" or "our") to Katherine Njuguna ("Borrower", "you" or "your") in connection with loan number 4717830084 and serves as the governing document for your loan account. Please keep this document for your records. This Schedule is provided for information purposes, and we modify the loan terms together with other information regarding your account.

1. GENERAL FEE TERMS
The fees described herein are assessed in accordance with the terms of your loan security instruments and applicable federal and state laws. Not every fee described herein may apply to your loan as those fees may vary based on your loan type.

Unless otherwise required by law or expressly stated in your loan documents, fees are earned when the performance is completed. Certain fees may vary and are subject to change. 

The Schedule is provided as a convenience and a general guide; it is not an endorsement of every charge that may be assessed during the life of your loan. The applicable fees may vary by jurisdiction, account type, and other criteria, including changes to state law. 

2. RETURNED PAYMENT CHARGES
If any payment for any reason is returned, a returned payment charge will be assessed unless waived by the financial institution where it was presented. This fee is also assessed on requests for funds that are not guaranteed by the financial institution. 

3. LATE CHARGES
When an established due date occurs, if the account remains unpaid after that period, we may assess a late charge if a late charge is specified in the loan documents. This charge applies to each payment that is not received in good funds. 

4. PAYOFF AND DEMAND STATEMENT FEES
Upon written request, we may require you to submit a statement to pay your loan in full. A request for a payoff statement may be subject to fees stipulated.
Upon full satisfaction of your loan, late fees, and other charges may not be included in a payoff statement; a payoff statement may also specify if payment is not received by the granting institution.

5. OPTIONAL EXPEDITED PAYMENT SERVICES  
As a consumer, you have the option of expedited payment services that allow you to make a payment by telephone, through online portals, or through a consumer-facing mobile app. If you choose to utilize these expedited payment services, please be aware that additional fees may be incurred. Fees may be modified, suspended, or waived at the discretion of the service providers.  
Where we offer an expedited payment service which is subject to a charge, the fee and available processing options are identified before you complete your payment and are governed by the applicable laws.  
However, the types of expedited payment options may be changed, and the available processing options, and the applicable fees associated with the expedited payment options, will be provided to you in advance.  
You should not assume that your account will not incur fees for similar services, as applicable fees may vary.  

6. PROPERTY INSPECTION AND PRESERVATION  
If you have become delinquent on your property securing your property from unsecured, abandoned, or inadequately maintained status, to the extent permitted by law, you may incur expenses related to the preservation of your property.  
These expenses include repairs to the property, property inspections to ensure proper upkeep and maintenance, and other services related to preserving and protecting the property. You will be responsible for these costs, and any charges incurred will be reflected on your account statement, as appropriate.  
Property inspections or preservation actions may be initiated by third-party vendors under your landlord authority and may vary with the location, conditions, and requirements of the properties involved and the local laws.  

7. FORCE-PLACED INSURANCE CHARGES  
Your homeowner’s insurance policy may be required by your mortgage contract. If you fail to maintain insurance on the property, we may be required to obtain insurance on the property. Such charges may be more expensive than the coverage you may choose yourself, and you will be responsible for these costs.  
We will provide notification of the forced placement of insurance and the respective coverage and costs. A cancellation of any forced-placed insurance must be presented to your servicer for review and acceptance as it will be subject to underwriting criteria.  

8. TAX-RELATED ADVANCES
